tool_name: Steel Beam Lateral Torsional Buckling Calculator - EN 1993 AISC 360
category: Structural Engineering / Steel Members / Beam Stability
standards: AISC Design Guide 9 - Torsional Analysis of Structural Steel Members
AISC Design Guide 28 - Stability Design of Steel Buildings
calculation_basis: Steel beam lateral torsional buckling screening using EN 1993-1-1 and AISC 360-22 style checks for major-axis bending resistance, lateral torsional buckling, compression flange unbraced length, moment gradient factor, elastic critical moment, EN non-dimensional slenderness and chi_LT reduction factor, AISC Lp/Lr/Cb-style flexural strength, compactness/local buckling review, bracing adequacy, load height effects, cantilever and monosymmetric section gates, axial-bending interaction gate, fire/seismic/fatigue gates and governing design bending resistance. The result is a preliminary engineering screening calculation and must not replace sealed structural design, global frame stability analysis, second-order analysis, bracing design, torsional restraint design, fire design, seismic design, fatigue design or authority approval.

inputs.name: Design Standard
inputs.validation_rule: Must be one of supported design standard modes.
inputs.note: Dual mode reports EN and AISC style strengths and selects the lower comparable bending resistance.

inputs.name: Beam Section Type
inputs.validation_rule: Must be a supported beam section type.
inputs.note: Closed tubular sections are generally not susceptible to classical LTB but still require bending/local buckling checks.

inputs.name: Section Symmetry
inputs.validation_rule: Must be a supported symmetry class.
inputs.note: Monosymmetric and asymmetric sections require specialist LTB/Mcr verification.

inputs.name: Bending Axis
inputs.validation_rule: Must be a supported bending axis mode.
inputs.note: Classical LTB usually applies to major-axis bending of laterally unrestrained beams.

inputs.name: Lateral Restraint Condition
inputs.validation_rule: Must be a supported lateral restraint condition.
inputs.note: Bracing strength, stiffness and connection details must be verified before restraint is credited.

inputs.name: Load Application Height
inputs.validation_rule: Must be a supported load-height condition.
inputs.note: Loads applied above the shear center can reduce LTB resistance and require Mcr helper correction.

inputs.name: Moment Diagram Type
inputs.validation_rule: Must be a supported moment diagram type.
inputs.note: Used for EN C1 and AISC Cb moment gradient factor.

inputs.name: EN Section Class
inputs.validation_rule: Must be a supported EN section class.
inputs.note: Class 4 requires effective section modulus and separate local buckling treatment.

inputs.name: AISC Compactness Class
inputs.validation_rule: Must be a supported AISC compactness class.
inputs.note: Noncompact/slender elements require local buckling reduction review.

inputs.name: Design Major-Axis Moment Demand
inputs.note: Use factored moment demand for EN/LRFD. For ASD mode use service-level demand consistent with ASD resistance.

inputs.name: Design Minor-Axis Moment Demand
inputs.note: Nonzero minor-axis moment triggers biaxial/beam-column review gate.

inputs.name: Design Shear Force
inputs.note: High shear can reduce bending resistance or trigger web/shear buckling review.

inputs.name: Design Axial Compression
inputs.note: Nonzero axial compression triggers beam-column interaction review gate.

inputs.name: Compression Flange Unbraced Length
inputs.note: Distance between effective lateral restraints of compression flange.

inputs.name: LTB Effective Length Factor
inputs.note: Accounts for boundary conditions and restraint idealization in Mcr/helper calculations.

inputs.name: Torsional Unbraced Length
inputs.note: Use same as unbraced length if no separate torsional restraint exists.

inputs.name: EN C1 Moment Gradient Factor
inputs.note: Use 0 to compute from moment diagram helper.

inputs.name: AISC Cb Moment Gradient Factor
inputs.note: Use 0 to compute from moment diagram helper. Final Cb must comply with selected AISC provisions.

inputs.name: End Moment A
inputs.note: Used for moment gradient factor calculation.

inputs.name: Quarter-Point Moment B
inputs.note: Used for AISC Cb and EN C1 helper.

inputs.name: Midspan Moment C
inputs.note: Used for AISC Cb and EN C1 helper.

inputs.name: Three-Quarter-Point Moment D
inputs.note: Used for AISC Cb and EN C1 helper.

inputs.name: End Moment E
inputs.note: Used for moment diagram and reverse-curvature review.

inputs.name: Gross Section Area
inputs.note: Gross cross-sectional area.

inputs.name: Plastic Section Modulus X
inputs.note: Required for EN Class 1/2 and AISC compact plastic moment calculations.

inputs.name: Elastic Section Modulus X
inputs.note: Elastic section modulus about major axis.

inputs.name: Effective Section Modulus X
inputs.note: Required for EN Class 4 or AISC slender sections.

inputs.name: AISC Elastic Section Modulus Sx
inputs.note: Use 0 to use elastic_section_modulus_x_mm3.

inputs.name: AISC Plastic Section Modulus Zx
inputs.note: Use 0 to use plastic_section_modulus_x_mm3.

inputs.name: Minor-Axis Moment of Inertia
inputs.note: Required for elastic critical moment and LTB.

inputs.name: Minor-Axis Radius of Gyration
inputs.note: Required for AISC Lp/Lr and LTB slenderness.

inputs.name: Torsional Constant
inputs.note: Required for open-section LTB calculations.

inputs.name: Warping Constant
inputs.note: Required for warping torsion and elastic critical moment.

inputs.name: AISC Effective Radius rts
inputs.note: Use 0 to compute by helper if section data are sufficient.

inputs.name: Distance Between Flange Centroids
inputs.note: Required for AISC Lr and LTB helper for I-shaped members.

inputs.name: Overall Section Depth
inputs.note: Used for section/detail review and load height effects.

inputs.name: Compression Flange Width
inputs.note: Used for compactness/local buckling helper.

inputs.name: Compression Flange Thickness
inputs.note: Used for compactness/local buckling helper.

inputs.name: Web Thickness
inputs.note: Used for compactness, shear and plate-girder review gates.

inputs.name: Steel Yield Strength
inputs.note: Steel yield strength.

inputs.name: Steel Ultimate Strength
inputs.note: Used for material traceability and review gates.

inputs.name: Elastic Modulus
inputs.note: Typical structural steel value is 210000 MPa.

inputs.name: Shear Modulus
inputs.note: Required for torsion and Mcr calculation.

inputs.name: EN LTB Buckling Curve
inputs.validation_rule: Must be a supported EN LTB curve.
inputs.note: LTB curve depends on section type, fabrication and national annex.

inputs.name: EN User LTB Imperfection Factor
inputs.note: Use 0 to map from EN LTB curve.

inputs.name: EN Beta Factor for LTB
inputs.note: Used in EN LTB reduction expression where applicable.

inputs.name: EN Partial Factor for Cross-Section Resistance
inputs.note: National annex may specify value.

inputs.name: EN Partial Factor for Buckling Resistance
inputs.note: National annex may specify value.

inputs.name: AISC LRFD Resistance Factor for Flexure
inputs.note: Used for AISC LRFD available flexural strength.

inputs.name: AISC ASD Safety Factor for Flexure
inputs.note: Used for AISC ASD allowable flexural strength.

inputs.name: Bracing Strength and Stiffness Verified
inputs.note: Unbraced length and restraint assumptions are invalid unless bracing strength/stiffness are verified.

inputs.name: Torsional Restraint Verified
inputs.note: Torsional bracing must prevent twist, not only lateral displacement.

inputs.name: Local Buckling Effective Properties Verified
inputs.note: Required for EN Class 4 or AISC slender sections.

inputs.name: Second-Order Analysis Completed
inputs.note: Frame stability and second-order effects must be checked outside simple member LTB capacity.

inputs.name: Cantilever or Unusual Boundary Condition
inputs.note: Cantilevers and unusual boundary conditions require specialist LTB/Mcr verification.

inputs.name: Seismic Design Required
inputs.note: True triggers seismic review gate.

inputs.name: Fire Design Required
inputs.note: True triggers fire resistance review gate.

inputs.name: Fatigue or Cyclic Loading Present
inputs.note: Fatigue/cyclic loading can govern details, bracing and connections.

inputs.name: Corrosion Protection Verified
inputs.note: Section loss can reduce section modulus, torsional constant and LTB resistance.

inputs.name: Licensed Structural Engineer Review Completed
inputs.note: Final member design must be reviewed and approved by competent structural engineer.

formulas.name: Selected EN Section Modulus
formulas.expression: section_class_en === 'CLASS_1' || section_class_en === 'CLASS_2' ? plastic_section_modulus_x_mm3 : (section_class_en === 'CLASS_3' ? elastic_section_modulus_x_mm3 : (section_class_en === 'CLASS_4' && effective_section_modulus_x_mm3 > 0 ? effective_section_modulus_x_mm3 : elastic_section_modulus_x_mm3))
formulas.reference: EN 1993 section modulus selection by section class
assumptions: Class 1/2 uses plastic section modulus.
Class 3 uses elastic section modulus.
Class 4 requires effective section modulus; otherwise review gate is triggered.
formulas.edge_cases: if (selected_en_section_modulus_mm3 <= 0) throw new Error('Selected EN section modulus must be positive')
formulas.cursor_instruction: Render as selected EN section modulus.

formulas.name: Selected AISC Sx
assumptions: Direct AISC Sx input is preferred.
Elastic section modulus is used when Sx is not entered.
formulas.edge_cases: if (selected_aisc_sx_mm3 <= 0) throw new Error('Selected AISC Sx must be positive')

formulas.name: Selected AISC Zx
assumptions: Direct AISC Zx input is preferred.
Plastic section modulus is used when Zx is not entered.
formulas.edge_cases: if (selected_aisc_zx_mm3 <= 0) throw new Error('Selected AISC Zx must be positive')

formulas.name: Effective LTB Length
assumptions: Effective length factor is externally determined.
Boundary and bracing assumptions require engineering verification.
formulas.edge_cases: if (ltb_effective_length_factor <= 0 || unbraced_length_mm <= 0) throw new Error('Effective LTB length inputs are invalid')

formulas.name: Moment Gradient Factor EN C1
formulas.expression: c1_moment_gradient_factor_en > 0 ? c1_moment_gradient_factor_en : computeEnC1MomentGradientFactor(moment_diagram_type, end_moment_a_knm, quarter_point_moment_b_knm, midspan_moment_c_knm, three_quarter_point_moment_d_knm, end_moment_e_knm)
formulas.reference: EN elastic critical moment moment-gradient factor
assumptions: User-entered C1 overrides helper.
Moment diagram helper is a screening method and must be verified for final design.
formulas.edge_cases: if (selected_en_c1_factor <= 0) throw new Error('EN C1 factor must be positive')

formulas.name: Moment Gradient Factor AISC Cb
formulas.expression: cb_moment_gradient_factor_aisc > 0 ? cb_moment_gradient_factor_aisc : computeAiscCbMomentGradientFactor(moment_diagram_type, end_moment_a_knm, quarter_point_moment_b_knm, midspan_moment_c_knm, three_quarter_point_moment_d_knm, end_moment_e_knm)
formulas.reference: AISC moment gradient factor Cb screening
assumptions: User-entered Cb overrides helper.
Moment diagram helper must match AISC requirements and unbraced segment moment values.
formulas.edge_cases: if (selected_aisc_cb_factor <= 0) throw new Error('AISC Cb factor must be positive')

formulas.name: Cross-Section Plastic Moment EN
assumptions: Section modulus is selected by EN class.
Shear reduction and local buckling beyond selected section class are not included here.
formulas.edge_cases: if (gamma_m0 < 1) throw new Error('gamma_M0 must be at least 1')

formulas.name: AISC Plastic Moment
assumptions: Plastic modulus is valid for compact sections.
Noncompact and slender sections require local buckling review.
formulas.edge_cases: if (aisc_plastic_moment_knm <= 0) throw new Error('AISC plastic moment must be positive')

formulas.name: AISC Yield Moment
assumptions: Elastic section modulus is used.
Used in AISC LTB transition branch.
formulas.edge_cases: if (aisc_yield_moment_knm <= 0) throw new Error('AISC yield moment must be positive')

formulas.name: Selected rts
assumptions: Direct section-table rts is preferred.
Helper computes rts only where sufficient section properties are available.
formulas.edge_cases: if (selected_rts_mm <= 0) throw new Error('Selected rts must be positive')

formulas.name: Elastic Critical Moment Mcr
formulas.expression: computeElasticCriticalMomentMcr(design_standard, beam_section_type, section_symmetry, effective_ltb_length_mm, torsional_unbraced_length_mm, selected_en_c1_factor, elastic_modulus_mpa, shear_modulus_mpa, moment_of_inertia_y_mm4, torsional_constant_j_mm4, warping_constant_cw_mm6, load_application_height, cantilever_or_unusual_boundary_condition)
formulas.reference: Elastic critical moment for lateral torsional buckling
assumptions: Mcr helper must implement selected boundary, loading-height and section assumptions.
Open sections require valid J and Cw.
Cantilevers and unusual boundary conditions require specialist verification.
formulas.edge_cases: if (elastic_critical_moment_mcr_knm <= 0) throw new Error('Elastic critical moment must be positive')

formulas.name: EN Non-Dimensional LTB Slenderness
assumptions: Elastic critical moment is calculated using compatible units and moment diagram assumptions.
Selected section modulus matches EN class.
formulas.edge_cases: if (elastic_critical_moment_mcr_knm <= 0) throw new Error('Mcr must be positive')

formulas.name: EN LTB Imperfection Factor
formulas.reference: EN LTB buckling curve imperfection factor
assumptions: User-defined alpha_LT overrides curve mapping.
LTB curve must be verified against section type and national annex.
formulas.edge_cases: if (en_ltb_imperfection_factor < 0) throw new Error('EN LTB imperfection factor cannot be negative')

formulas.name: EN LTB Phi Factor
assumptions: Beta factor is user-configurable for national annex/project method.
Expression is used for screening and must match selected EN method.
formulas.edge_cases: if (en_ltb_phi_factor <= 0) throw new Error('EN LTB phi factor must be positive')

formulas.name: EN LTB Reduction Factor chi_LT
assumptions: Reduction factor is limited to maximum 1.0.
Negative square-root term is clamped only for numerical noise.
formulas.edge_cases: if (en_chi_lt <= 0 || en_chi_lt > 1) throw new Error('EN chi_LT out of range')

formulas.name: EN LTB Design Bending Resistance
formulas.reference: EN lateral torsional buckling bending resistance
assumptions: chi_LT reduction is applied to selected EN section modulus.
gamma_M1 is applied.
formulas.edge_cases: if (gamma_m1 < 1) throw new Error('gamma_M1 must be at least 1')

formulas.name: AISC Limiting Unbraced Length Lp
formulas.reference: AISC limiting laterally unbraced length for full plastic bending screening
assumptions: Screening expression for I-shaped members.
Final AISC design must verify section-specific applicability.
formulas.edge_cases: if (radius_gyration_y_mm <= 0 || yield_strength_mpa <= 0) throw new Error('AISC Lp inputs invalid')

formulas.name: AISC Limiting Unbraced Length Lr
formulas.reference: AISC limiting laterally unbraced length for inelastic-to-elastic LTB transition
assumptions: Helper must implement selected AISC section-specific Lr expression.
Requires valid Sx, rts, J and h0 for I-shaped members.
formulas.edge_cases: if (aisc_lr_mm <= 0) throw new Error('AISC Lr must be positive')

formulas.name: AISC Nominal Flexural Strength for LTB
formulas.expression: computeAiscLtbNominalMoment(beam_section_type, aisc_compactness_class, effective_ltb_length_mm, aisc_lp_mm, aisc_lr_mm, selected_aisc_cb_factor, aisc_plastic_moment_knm, aisc_yield_moment_knm, elastic_modulus_mpa, selected_aisc_sx_mm3, selected_rts_mm, torsional_constant_j_mm4, distance_between_flange_centroids_h0_mm)
formulas.reference: AISC Chapter F style LTB nominal flexural strength screening
assumptions: Helper applies compact/noncompact/slender and Lb/Lp/Lr branch logic.
Nominal moment must not exceed applicable plastic/local buckling cap.
Final AISC design must verify section-specific provisions.
formulas.edge_cases: if (aisc_ltb_nominal_flexural_strength_knm <= 0) throw new Error('AISC LTB nominal flexural strength must be positive')
formulas.cursor_instruction: Render AISC nominal LTB flexural strength.

formulas.name: AISC Available Flexural Strength
formulas.dimensional_check: kN*m × phi or kN*m / omega = kN*m
assumptions: LRFD applies phi_b.
ASD applies Omega_b.
formulas.edge_cases: if (aisc_phi_b <= 0 || aisc_omega_b < 1) throw new Error('AISC flexure factors invalid')

formulas.name: Selected Design LTB Bending Resistance
formulas.reference: Design standard LTB bending resistance selection
assumptions: Dual-code mode uses lower comparable bending resistance.
Owner specification mode requires separate review.
formulas.edge_cases: if (selected_design_ltb_bending_resistance_knm <= 0) throw new Error('Selected LTB bending resistance must be positive')
formulas.cursor_instruction: Render selected design LTB bending resistance.

formulas.name: LTB Moment Utilization
assumptions: Demand and resistance basis are consistent with selected design standard.
Major-axis bending governs LTB screening.
formulas.edge_cases: if (selected_design_ltb_bending_resistance_knm <= 0) throw new Error('LTB resistance must be positive')

formulas.name: Cross-Section Bending Utilization
formulas.expression: design_standard === 'EN_1993_1_1' ? design_major_axis_moment_knm / en_cross_section_bending_resistance_knm : design_major_axis_moment_knm / (design_standard === 'AISC_360_ASD' ? aisc_plastic_moment_knm / aisc_omega_b : aisc_phi_b * aisc_plastic_moment_knm)
assumptions: Cross-section resistance is checked separately from LTB.
Local buckling and compactness gates can override this result.
formulas.edge_cases: if (!isFinite(cross_section_bending_utilization)) throw new Error('Cross-section bending utilization invalid')

formulas.name: LTB Governing Branch
formulas.expression: identifyLtbGoverningBranch({EN_LTB: en_ltb_design_bending_resistance_knm, AISC_LTB: aisc_available_flexural_strength_knm, EN_CROSS_SECTION: en_cross_section_bending_resistance_knm})
assumptions: Lowest relevant bending resistance branch governs.
Null or not-applicable branches are ignored by helper.
formulas.edge_cases: if (!ltb_governing_branch) throw new Error('LTB governing branch cannot be determined')
formulas.uncertainty_expression: Classification confidence depends on resistance uncertainty.

formulas.name: Local Buckling and Compactness Status
formulas.reference: Local buckling and compactness review gate
assumptions: Class 4/slender sections require verified effective properties.
Class 3/noncompact sections require local buckling reduction review.
formulas.edge_cases: if (!local_buckling_status) throw new Error('Local buckling status cannot be determined')

formulas.name: Bracing Status
formulas.reference: Lateral bracing and compression flange restraint review gate
assumptions: Bracing must have adequate strength and stiffness.
Compression flange restraint must be continuous or discrete as assumed.
formulas.edge_cases: if (typeof bracing_strength_stiffness_verified !== 'boolean') throw new Error('Bracing verification flag must be boolean')

formulas.name: Torsional Restraint Status
assumptions: Lateral bracing alone may not provide torsional restraint.
Top-flange destabilizing loads require careful restraint review.
formulas.edge_cases: if (typeof torsional_restraint_verified !== 'boolean') throw new Error('Torsional restraint flag must be boolean')

formulas.name: Section Applicability Status
assumptions: Standard LTB expressions are strongest for doubly symmetric I/H sections.
Closed sections have low classical LTB susceptibility but still require bending/local checks.
formulas.edge_cases: if (!section_applicability_status) throw new Error('Section applicability status cannot be determined')

formulas.name: Load Height Status
formulas.reference: Load height and destabilizing load review gate
assumptions: Destabilizing top-flange load can reduce Mcr.
Unknown load height is not acceptable for final LTB design.
formulas.edge_cases: if (!load_height_status) throw new Error('Load height status cannot be determined')

formulas.name: Boundary Condition Status
assumptions: Cantilevers and unusual boundary conditions require specialist Mcr and restraint verification.
Standard simply supported/fork support assumptions are not universal.
formulas.edge_cases: if (typeof cantilever_or_unusual_boundary_condition !== 'boolean') throw new Error('Boundary condition flag must be boolean')

formulas.name: Shear Interaction Review Status
formulas.reference: Shear and bending interaction review gate
assumptions: High shear can reduce bending resistance or trigger web shear buckling.
This tool does not perform full shear design.
formulas.edge_cases: if (design_shear_force_kn < 0) throw new Error('Design shear cannot be negative')

formulas.name: Axial-Bending Interaction Status
assumptions: This tool calculates LTB bending resistance.
Axial force or biaxial bending requires a beam-column interaction module.
formulas.edge_cases: if (design_axial_compression_kn < 0 || design_minor_axis_moment_knm < 0) throw new Error('Axial compression and minor-axis moment cannot be negative')

formulas.name: Second-Order Stability Status
formulas.reference: Frame stability and second-order analysis review gate
assumptions: Member LTB capacity alone does not verify frame stability.
P-Delta, sway and global imperfections require structural analysis review.
formulas.edge_cases: if (typeof second_order_analysis_completed !== 'boolean') throw new Error('Second-order flag must be boolean')

formulas.name: Special Review Status
formulas.reference: Seismic, fire and fatigue review gates
assumptions: Seismic, fire and fatigue/cyclic loading require specialist checks.
Ambient-temperature static LTB resistance is not sufficient for these cases.
formulas.edge_cases: if (typeof seismic_design_required !== 'boolean' || typeof fire_design_required !== 'boolean' || typeof fatigue_or_cyclic_loading_present !== 'boolean') throw new Error('Special review flags must be boolean')

formulas.name: Durability Status
assumptions: Corrosion loss can reduce section modulus, torsional constant and LTB capacity.
Durability is not calculated unless reduced section properties are entered.
formulas.edge_cases: if (typeof corrosion_protection_verified !== 'boolean') throw new Error('Durability flag must be boolean')

formulas.name: LTB Strength Status
formulas.reference: LTB and cross-section bending strength status
assumptions: Utilization limit is 1.0 for screening.
Cross-section bending and LTB are both checked.
formulas.edge_cases: if (ltb_moment_utilization < 0 || cross_section_bending_utilization < 0) throw new Error('Utilization cannot be negative')
formulas.uncertainty_expression: Classification confidence depends on utilization uncertainty.

formulas.name: Governing Utilization
assumptions: The maximum of LTB and cross-section bending utilization governs numeric screening.
Review gates can override numeric acceptance.
formulas.edge_cases: if (!isFinite(governing_utilization)) throw new Error('Governing utilization is invalid')

formulas.name: Governing Limit State
assumptions: Largest utilization controls numeric screening.
Special review gates are evaluated separately.
formulas.edge_cases: if (!governing_limit_state) throw new Error('Governing limit state cannot be determined')
formulas.uncertainty_expression: Classification confidence depends on utilization uncertainty.

formulas.name: Engineer Review Status
assumptions: Final structural member design requires competent engineer review.
Software output is not a sealed calculation.
formulas.edge_cases: if (typeof licensed_engineer_review_completed !== 'boolean') throw new Error('Engineer review flag must be boolean')

formulas.name: Beam LTB Risk Class
formulas.expression: ltb_strength_status === 'LTB_OR_BENDING_STRENGTH_NOT_ACCEPTABLE' ? 'LTB_OR_BENDING_STRENGTH_NOT_ACCEPTABLE' : (local_buckling_status !== 'LOCAL_BUCKLING_ACCEPTABLE_FOR_SCREENING' ? local_buckling_status : (section_applicability_status !== 'SECTION_APPLICABLE_FOR_STANDARD_LTB_SCREENING' ? section_applicability_status : (bracing_status.includes('REVIEW_REQUIRED') ? 'BRACING_RESTRAINT_REVIEW_REQUIRED' : (torsional_restraint_status === 'TORSIONAL_RESTRAINT_REVIEW_REQUIRED' ? torsional_restraint_status : (load_height_status !== 'LOAD_HEIGHT_ACCEPTABLE_FOR_SCREENING' ? load_height_status : (boundary_condition_status !== 'BOUNDARY_CONDITION_ACCEPTABLE_FOR_SCREENING' ? boundary_condition_status : (axial_bending_interaction_status !== 'PURE_MAJOR_AXIS_BENDING_SCREENING' ? axial_bending_interaction_status : (second_order_stability_status !== 'SECOND_ORDER_ANALYSIS_COMPLETED' ? second_order_stability_status : (special_review_status !== 'NO_SPECIAL_REVIEW_TRIGGERED' ? special_review_status : (durability_status !== 'DURABILITY_ACCEPTABLE_FOR_SCREENING' ? durability_status : (engineer_review_status !== 'LICENSED_ENGINEER_REVIEW_COMPLETED' ? engineer_review_status : 'CONTROLLED_BEAM_LTB_SCREENING')))))))))))
assumptions: Strength, local buckling, section applicability, bracing, torsional restraint, load height, boundary condition, interaction, second-order, special review, durability and engineer gates are evaluated together.
Review gates can override numerical acceptance.
formulas.edge_cases: if (!beam_ltb_risk_class) throw new Error('Beam LTB risk class cannot be determined')
formulas.uncertainty_expression: Classification confidence depends on moment demand, unbraced length, Cb/C1, Mcr, section properties, bracing, torsional restraint and review gates.

formulas.name: Decision
formulas.expression: ltb_strength_status === 'LTB_OR_BENDING_STRENGTH_NOT_ACCEPTABLE' ? 'BEAM_LTB_OR_BENDING_CAPACITY_NOT_ACCEPTABLE' : (local_buckling_status !== 'LOCAL_BUCKLING_ACCEPTABLE_FOR_SCREENING' ? local_buckling_status : (section_applicability_status !== 'SECTION_APPLICABLE_FOR_STANDARD_LTB_SCREENING' ? section_applicability_status : (bracing_status.includes('REVIEW_REQUIRED') ? bracing_status : (torsional_restraint_status === 'TORSIONAL_RESTRAINT_REVIEW_REQUIRED' ? torsional_restraint_status : (load_height_status !== 'LOAD_HEIGHT_ACCEPTABLE_FOR_SCREENING' ? load_height_status : (boundary_condition_status !== 'BOUNDARY_CONDITION_ACCEPTABLE_FOR_SCREENING' ? boundary_condition_status : (axial_bending_interaction_status !== 'PURE_MAJOR_AXIS_BENDING_SCREENING' ? axial_bending_interaction_status : (second_order_stability_status !== 'SECOND_ORDER_ANALYSIS_COMPLETED' ? second_order_stability_status : (special_review_status !== 'NO_SPECIAL_REVIEW_TRIGGERED' ? special_review_status : (durability_status !== 'DURABILITY_ACCEPTABLE_FOR_SCREENING' ? durability_status : (engineer_review_status !== 'LICENSED_ENGINEER_REVIEW_COMPLETED' ? engineer_review_status : 'BEAM_LTB_CAPACITY_ACCEPTABLE_FOR_SCREENING')))))))))))
formulas.reference: Integrated steel beam LTB decision rule
assumptions: Final acceptance requires correct code basis, section classification, moment diagram, unbraced length, bracing design, torsional restraint, local buckling review, second-order analysis and structural engineer approval.
Screening acceptance does not authorize construction, fabrication, member substitution or bracing removal.
formulas.edge_cases: if (!decision) throw new Error('Decision cannot be determined')
formulas.uncertainty_expression: Decision confidence depends on design moment, unbraced length, section properties, Mcr, moment gradient factor, local buckling, bracing verification, torsional restraint and engineer review.

outputs.name: Beam LTB Decision
outputs.uncertainty: Decision confidence depends on design moment, unbraced length, section properties, Mcr, moment gradient factor, local buckling, bracing verification, torsional restraint and engineer review

outputs.name: Beam LTB Risk Class
outputs.uncertainty: Classification confidence depends on moment demand, unbraced length, Cb/C1, Mcr, section properties, bracing, torsional restraint and review gates

outputs.name: Selected Design LTB Bending Resistance
outputs.uncertainty: u_c ≈ selected design LTB bending resistance × 0.45

outputs.name: Governing Limit State
outputs.uncertainty: Classification confidence depends on utilization uncertainty

outputs.name: LTB Governing Branch
outputs.uncertainty: Classification confidence depends on resistance uncertainty

outputs.name: LTB Strength Status
outputs.uncertainty: Classification confidence depends on utilization uncertainty

rules.message: Design moment, shear and axial compression demands cannot be negative.
Unbraced lengths and LTB effective length factor must be positive.
Moment gradient factors cannot be negative.
Section area and section modulus inputs are invalid.
Minor-axis inertia and radius of gyration must be positive.
Torsional and warping section properties cannot be negative.
Elastic and shear modulus must be positive.
EN LTB factors or partial factors are invalid.
AISC flexure phi/omega factors are invalid.
EN Class 4 section requires verified effective section modulus.
AISC slender section requires effective section modulus or local buckling reduction helper.
I/H section LTB requires valid J and Cw for reliable Mcr calculation.
Bracing is not verified. Unbraced length assumption may be invalid.
Destabilizing top-flange loading without torsional restraint requires specialist review.

smart_warnings.message: Licensed structural engineer review is not completed. Do not use result for construction or fabrication.
Design moment exceeds LTB or cross-section bending resistance.
Local buckling or section compactness review is required.
Bracing strength/stiffness is not verified. LTB capacity may be unconservative.
Section shape requires specialist LTB/Mcr verification.
Load height may reduce LTB resistance. Verify destabilizing load effects.
Cantilever or unusual boundary condition requires specialist LTB verification.
Axial compression or biaxial bending detected. Use beam-column interaction module.
Second-order stability analysis is not completed.
Seismic design required. Ductility, compactness, bracing and system checks are outside this tool.
Fire design required. Ambient-temperature LTB capacity is not sufficient.
Corrosion protection is not verified. Section loss may reduce LTB capacity.
Governing utilization is above 0.90. Review Mcr, bracing, Cb/C1, section properties and load height effects.

engine_rules.error_propagation: ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM Type A/B. Report u_c(y) for effective LTB length, C1, Cb, section modulus, Mcr, EN slenderness, chi_LT, EN LTB resistance, AISC Lp, AISC Lr, AISC nominal strength, AISC available strength, selected resistance, moment utilization and governing utilization. Use root-sum-square propagation for direct formulas and scenario/Monte Carlo propagation for Mcr and moment gradient helpers where available. Flag high uncertainty when bracing is not verified, load height is destabilizing, Cb/C1 is estimated, J or Cw is missing, section is monosymmetric/asymmetric, boundary conditions are unusual or local buckling effective properties are not verified.
engine_rules.fmea: Required if governing_utilization > 0.90, bracing_strength_stiffness_verified = false, torsional_restraint_verified = false for torsional bracing cases, load_application_height = TOP_FLANGE_DESTABILIZING, section_applicability_status is special review, section_class_en = CLASS_4, aisc_compactness_class = SLENDER, cantilever_or_unusual_boundary_condition = true, seismic_design_required = true, fire_design_required = true, or beam supports life-safety/public-access/production-critical systems. RPN = S×O×D.
engine_rules.audit_log: ISO 9001 traceable fields: design standard, section type, section symmetry, bending axis, restraint condition, load height, moment diagram type, EN section class, AISC compactness class, moment demand, shear demand, axial demand, unbraced length, effective length factor, torsional length, C1, Cb, moment values, section moduli, Iy, ry, J, Cw, rts, h0, section dimensions, material strengths, E, G, EN LTB curve, alpha_LT, beta, gamma factors, AISC phi/omega, bracing flag, torsional restraint flag, local buckling flag, second-order flag, boundary flag, seismic flag, fire flag, fatigue flag, corrosion flag, engineer review flag, all formula outputs, warnings, decision, formula version, timestamp and input hash.

helpers_required.name: computeEnC1MomentGradientFactor
helpers_required.signature: computeEnC1MomentGradientFactor(momentDiagramType: string, MA: number, MB: number, MC: number, MD: number, ME: number): number
helpers_required.description: Computes EN C1 moment-gradient factor from moment diagram type and segment moment values using project-configured method.

helpers_required.name: computeAiscCbMomentGradientFactor
helpers_required.signature: computeAiscCbMomentGradientFactor(momentDiagramType: string, MA: number, MB: number, MC: number, MD: number, ME: number): number
helpers_required.description: Computes AISC Cb factor from moment values at required points in the unbraced segment.

helpers_required.name: computeElasticCriticalMomentMcr
helpers_required.signature: computeElasticCriticalMomentMcr(designStandard: string, sectionType: string, symmetry: string, LbEffMm: number, LtMm: number, C1: number, E: number, G: number, Iy: number, J: number, Cw: number, loadHeight: string, unusualBoundary: boolean): number
helpers_required.description: Computes elastic critical moment for lateral torsional buckling with boundary, load-height, torsion and warping assumptions.

helpers_required.name: mapEnLtbCurveToImperfectionFactor
helpers_required.description: Maps EN LTB curve to imperfection factor using project-configured EN table values.

helpers_required.name: computeAiscRts
helpers_required.signature: computeAiscRts(IyMm4: number, CwMm6: number, SxMm3: number): number
helpers_required.description: Computes AISC rts where sufficient section properties are available.

helpers_required.name: computeAiscLrLimitLength
helpers_required.signature: computeAiscLrLimitLength(E: number, Fy: number, Sx: number, rts: number, J: number, h0: number): number
helpers_required.description: Computes AISC Lr limiting unbraced length for LTB transition using selected section-specific provisions.

helpers_required.name: computeAiscLtbNominalMoment
helpers_required.signature: computeAiscLtbNominalMoment(sectionType: string, compactness: string, LbEff: number, Lp: number, Lr: number, Cb: number, Mp: number, My: number, E: number, Sx: number, rts: number, J: number, h0: number): number
helpers_required.description: Computes AISC nominal flexural strength for LTB with compactness and Lb/Lp/Lr branch logic.

helpers_required.name: identifyLtbGoverningBranch
helpers_required.description: Returns the lowest applicable bending resistance branch.

helpers_required.name: identifyGoverningLimitState
helpers_required.description: Returns the limit state with maximum evaluated utilization.

helpers_required.name: max
helpers_required.description: Returns maximum finite numeric value while ignoring nulls.

reference_code.validation: Zod schema. Fail closed on invalid moment/shear/axial demands, invalid unbraced lengths, invalid LTB effective length factor, invalid moment gradient factors, invalid section properties, invalid dimensions, invalid material strengths, invalid elastic/shear modulus, invalid EN factors, invalid AISC phi/omega factors and invalid boolean review gates.
reference_code.testing: Jest cases: compact W-shape fully braced, compact W-shape Lb less than Lp, inelastic LTB between Lp and Lr, elastic LTB beyond Lr, EN Class 1/2 section, EN Class 3 section, EN Class 4 without effective modulus warning, AISC slender section warning, dual-code envelope selection, user-entered Cb override, computed Cb from moment points, user-entered C1 override, computed C1 from moment diagram, top-flange destabilizing load warning, bracing missing gate, torsional restraint missing gate, monosymmetric section gate, closed tube low LTB susceptibility gate, cantilever boundary gate, axial compression interaction gate, fire gate, seismic gate, engineer review missing, final acceptable screening decision.
implementation_notes: Do not use eval(). Use existing AST/helper expression engine.
Use deterministic formula order F1 to F41.
Use SI-primary units: kN, kN*m, mm, mm2, mm3, mm4, mm6, MPa and ratio.
Use null for not-applicable specialist checks, not Infinity.
Do not embed copyrighted EN/AISC tables. EN LTB curve, AISC compactness and section-specific branch helpers must use project-configurable traceable parameters.
Dual-code envelope mode must report EN and AISC intermediate values and select the lower comparable design LTB bending resistance.
Mcr calculation must expose assumptions for boundary conditions, load height, warping restraint and torsional restraint.
Closed HSS/box/pipe sections must not be treated as automatically safe; they should bypass classical LTB only where appropriate and still pass cross-section/local buckling checks.
Class 4 EN sections and AISC slender sections must not silently use gross/plastic properties unless effective properties are verified.
Bracing strength and stiffness verification is mandatory before reduced unbraced length is credited.
Nonzero axial force or biaxial bending must trigger beam-column interaction review.
Seismic and fire flags must block final acceptability and require specialist design modules.

Decision enum values: BEAM_LTB_CAPACITY_ACCEPTABLE_FOR_SCREENING, BEAM_LTB_OR_BENDING_CAPACITY_NOT_ACCEPTABLE, LOCAL_BUCKLING_EFFECTIVE_PROPERTIES_REVIEW_REQUIRED, LOCAL_BUCKLING_REDUCTION_REVIEW_REQUIRED, SPECIAL_SECTION_LTB_REVIEW_REQUIRED, CLOSED_SECTION_LTB_LOW_SUSCEPTIBILITY_REVIEW, BRACING_RESTRAINT_REVIEW_REQUIRED, DISCRETE_BRACING_REVIEW_REQUIRED, TORSIONAL_RESTRAINT_REVIEW_REQUIRED, LOAD_HEIGHT_LTB_REVIEW_REQUIRED, CANTILEVER_OR_UNUSUAL_LTB_BOUNDARY_REVIEW_REQUIRED, BEAM_COLUMN_INTERACTION_REVIEW_REQUIRED, SECOND_ORDER_ANALYSIS_REVIEW_REQUIRED, SEISMIC_BEAM_STABILITY_REVIEW_REQUIRED, FIRE_RESISTANCE_REVIEW_REQUIRED, FATIGUE_OR_CYCLIC_LOADING_REVIEW_REQUIRED, DURABILITY_REVIEW_REQUIRED, LICENSED_ENGINEER_REVIEW_REQUIRED.

tuv_checklist: Elastic critical moment Mcr helper implemented
EN LTB imperfection factor mapping implemented
EN LTB design bending resistance implemented
AISC LTB nominal moment helper implemented
AISC LRFD/ASD available flexural strength implemented
Local buckling and compactness gate implemented
FMEA required for high-utilization, unverified bracing, destabilizing load height, Class 4/slender sections, special sections, cantilevers, seismic and fire cases
Audit hash generated for every successful calculation
No hidden defaults for design moment, unbraced length, section modulus, material strength, Mcr, Cb/C1, bracing verification, torsional restraint verification or engineer review gates
metadata.calibration_trace: Design moments, shear and axial force from structural analysis/load combinations; unbraced lengths from bracing layout and frame model; Cb/C1 from moment diagram; section properties from certified section table, mill data or section analysis; material strengths from mill certificate; EN section class from section classification; AISC compactness from AISC classification; bracing and torsional restraint verification from bracing/connection design; final approval from engineer of record.
source_basis: EN 1993-1-1 lateral torsional buckling and bending member design context
AISC 360-22 Chapter F flexural member and lateral torsional buckling context
AISC Design Guide 9 torsional analysis context
AISC Design Guide 28 stability design context
ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM uncertainty reporting basis
limitations: This calculator is a lateral torsional buckling screening tool, not a sealed structural calculation, finite-element model, global frame stability analysis, bracing design, torsional restraint design, fire design, seismic design or fabrication approval.
Mcr is highly sensitive to boundary condition, warping restraint, load height, moment diagram, lateral restraint and torsional restraint assumptions.
Class 4 EN sections and AISC slender sections require verified effective properties.
Monosymmetric, asymmetric, plate girder, tapered, curved, cellular, castellated, haunched, cantilever and unusual support cases may require specialist LTB analysis.
Nonzero axial compression, biaxial bending or high shear requires separate interaction checks.
Final use requires competent structural engineer approval and project-specific code compliance.
metadata.cursor_instruction: Integrate this tool into the existing SectorCalc PRO tool architecture without creating a new UI framework. Use current tool-card, section-property input pattern, moment-diagram input pattern, LTB mode diagram panel, results panel, formula panel, warning panel, chart panel, table panel and audit log components.
